name: Haggis#1
recipeIngredient: Liver and lights of a sheep
recipeInstructions:
1. Thoroughly wash the stomach bag in cold water. Turn it inside out and scald it, then scrape the surface with a knife.
2. Soak the bag in cold salted water overnight.
3. Remove the bag from the water and set aside while preparing the filling.
4. Wash the pluck.
5. Put the pluck into a pan with the windpipe hanging over the side into a bowl to let out any impurities.
6. Cover the pluck with cold water, add 1 teaspoon of salt, and bring to a boil. Skim the surface, then simmer for 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
7. Parboil the onions, drain, and chop them roughly. Also toast the pinhead oatmeal until golden brown.
8. Drain the pluck when ready and cut away the windpipe and any excess gristle.
9. Mince half the liver with all the heart and lights, then stir in the shredded suet, toasted oatmeal, and onions. Season well with salt and pepper.
10. Moisten the mixture with as much of the onion or pluck water as necessary to make it soft.
11. Fill the bag just over half full with the prepared filling, leaving room for swelling during cooking, and sew the ends together using the trussing needle and fine string.
12. Prick the bag in several places with the needle.
13. Place the haggis on an enamel plate and put it into a pan of boiling water. Cover the pan and cook for about 3 hours, adding more boiling water as necessary to keep the haggis covered.
14. Serve with the traditional accompaniment of Tatties-an'Neeps (mashed potatoes and mashed turnips).
